verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a serine protease of the kallikrein family, primarily expressed in prostate tissue. While the NCBI summary emphasizes its intracellular role in pro-PSA activation, kallikreins are secretory proteases that reach the extracellular space and can access the cell surface via secretory trafficking. KLK2 is documented to be secreted and detectable in seminal plasma and serum, and as a protease it can engage extracellular substrates. The protein body reaches the outer face via its own secretory mechanism (no stable membrane anchor of its own, but reaches extracellular space and can interact with surface-displayed or shed substrates). This qualifies as contextual surface accessibility — the protein is not stably membrane-resident but does reach the extracellular face during its normal lifecycle and is accessible to extracellular binders.
